items.title: Sydney healthcare workers push for refugee Medicare access amid growing health concerns

items.title: Jewellery shop targeted in second alleged arson on Gold Coast in two days
items.summary: The latest incident comes a day after a car was allegedly rammed into a Gold Coast car wash business and set alight.

items.title: 'We've been calling for a de-escalation': PM welcomes ceasefire — as it happened
items.summary: Anthony Albanese has described the two-week US-Iran ceasefire as positive news but says Donald Trump's rhetoric overnight wasn't appropriate.

items.title: Leg amputee forced to wait two months for rehabilitation services in Tamworth
items.summary: After travelling more than 2,000 kilometres across four separate hospitals, Phillip Norris says staff shortages in hospitals "needs to change".

items.title: Phillip Norris walks on new leg
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Hobby beekeepers urged to check for varroa mite as more than 1,000 ACT beehives destroyed
items.summary: Experts say half of the ACT's 6,000 beehives have been impacted by the fast-spreading parasite varroa mite with more than 1,000 destroyed.

items.title: Perth parents found guilty of starving teenage daughter granted bail ahead of possible retrial
items.summary: A Perth man and woman who were found guilty in 2024 of starving their daughter, who weighed about 28 kilograms when she was admitted to hospital aged nearly 17, have their convictions set aside on appeal.

items.title: Donald Trump sends JD Vance to save far-right Hungarian PM Viktor Orbán
items.summary: US President Donald Trump has sent his vice-president to Hungary in a bid to salvage the political future of far-right leader Viktor Orbán, who is flailing in the polls days before a closely watched election.

items.title: Alex de Minaur defeats Cameron Norrie at Monte Carlo Masters round of 32
items.summary: Alex de Minaur is through to the final 16 of the Monte-Carlo Masters after defeating Britain's Cameron Norrie.

items.title: Darwin midwife's $1.1 million NT government contract under review
items.summary: Darwin midwife Claire Marks was under investigation by the nursing and midwifery watchdog when she was handed a $1.1 million contract by the NT government, which is now reviewing the matter, as doctors continue to raise concerns, and the service brings on new staff.

items.title: SA authorities confirm Port Lincoln tuna deaths not caused by harmful algal bloom
items.summary: Environmental authorities have found the culprit for a mass fish mortality event off the coast of Port Lincoln, saying it is unrelated to the state's harmful algal bloom.

items.title: Gippsland farmers angered by plans to turn Hazelwood coal mine into huge lake
items.summary: There are plans to turn the former Hazelwood coal mine in Victoria into a lake bigger than Sydney Harbour, but farmers and environmentalists are worried about the impact on nearby waterways.

items.title: Category five Cyclone Maila slowly moving towards Far North Queensland, tourists urged not to cancel travel plans
items.summary: Severe Tropical Cyclone Maila is now a category five system and is expected to cross into Far North Queensland on Sunday.

items.title: Child safety legislation change ensures mandatory child abuse reporting from Tasmania Police
items.summary: The child safety regulator sought a law change after receiving "concerning" correspondence from Tasmania Police, which said its officers were not bound by a key child protection measure.

items.title: National park expansion fuels land price surge and anxiety of NSW farmers
items.summary: Hailed as a win for the environment, $200 million has been spent purchasing new national parks in outback NSW — but not everyone is celebrating.

items.title: Matt Canavan's 'Australia on steroids' economic plan looks to the stars, manufacturing and a baby boom
items.summary: A domestic space industry, a baby boom, new dams and more factories are on Nationals leader Matt Canavan's to-do list to deliver what he describes as an Australian economic revolution.

items.title: Cost of filling up Australia's best-selling car increases by $100 since start of Iran war
items.summary: The ABC analyses how the cost of a full tank in Australia's most-purchased cars of 2025 has changed since the beginning of the Iran war.

items.title: PM urged to intervene in youth justice systems to break the cycle of crime
items.summary: Hundreds of legal, health and children's organisations are calling on Anthony Albanese to convene an emergency summit to address the over-incarceration of Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander children.

items.title: NDIS provider registrations in spotlight as Labor eyes options to make budget savings
items.summary: Stricter registration rules would weed out low-quality NDIS providers while delivering better services and budget savings, major operators say.

items.title: The grief of being estranged from family
items.summary: Estrangement usually begins after a significant traumatic experience with a loved one, experts say.

items.title: Boards make decisions affecting the nation but they don't look like Australia
items.summary: There's a push to get younger people, women and more diverse community members on the boards making the big decisions in Australian life.

items.title: Elite women athletes question their future in Australian sport as pay lags behind expectations
items.summary: Recently retired rugby union player Mel Kawa regularly broke down in tears, grappling with her love of the game and the $2,000 she was paid to play it.

items.title: How Australia's most decorated soldier, Ben Roberts-Smith, ended up facing war crime charges
items.summary: During his years-long battle to clear his name, a separate joint investigation stretching 9,000 kilometres was discreetly transpiring.

items.title: Sword of the Sea is the Grammy Award-winning follow-up from the team behind Journey
items.summary: Art director Matt Nava and composer Austin Wintory reveal how their cult 2012 video game Journey shaped their latest offering, the Grammy Award-winning Sword of the Sea.

items.title: Travel quiz, April 8: Pack your suitcase, we're going on a trip
items.summary: Which European microstate is smaller? And which US president isn’t on Mount Rushmore?

items.title: Trump agrees to 'double-sided ceasefire' with Iran less than 90 minutes before deadline
items.summary: Donald Trump says he has agreed to suspend US attacks on Iran for two weeks as part of a "double-sided ceasefire". Iran is claiming victory and says it will stop its attacks if the US does.

items.title: Fiji flight from Sydney diverted after tropical cyclone thwarts three landing attempts
items.summary: A Fiji Airways flight from Sydney had to divert after bad weather from a tropical cyclone thwarted its attempts to land.

items.summary: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

items.title: Former Australian Test cricketer David Warner facing drink driving charges in Sydney
items.summary: Former Australian Test cricketer David Warner is charged with mid-range drink driving in Sydney's east over the weekend.

items.title: POLAIR vision of the arrest of 39-year-old man following a carjacking spree
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Man accused of three-day armed carjacking spree across southern Queensland appears in court
items.summary: Police allege Beau Wallace Flegler, 39, stole multiple vehicles at gunpoint across the Easter long weekend before his arrest in East Toowoomba.

items.title: Tuesday finance with Alan Kohler
items.summary: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

items.title: Man sentenced over fatal hit and run of teenager Bryson Dimovski near Newcastle
items.summary: The father of a teenager who was fatally struck while riding an e-scooter near Newcastle says the consequences of driving while distracted are catastrophic.

items.title: Super Netball Round-Up: Netball ahead of the curve on sports gambling and the pending ownership of the Giants
items.summary: The ex-CEO of Netball Australia said it was behind the times when she walked out the door in December 2023, but new gambling reforms announced by the federal government show the sport was right to steer clear from sports betting.

items.title: Wallabies player Josh Flook speaks about move to Italy after Super Rugby Pacific season
items.summary: Josh Flook has elaborated on the lifelong criticism that prompted his move to Italy and defended incoming Wallabies coach Les Kiss as crunch time looms for the Queensland Reds.

items.title: Maree Mavis Crabtree pleads not guilty to murdering son Jonathan with spiked fruit smoothie
items.summary: Maree Mavis Crabtree has pleaded not guilty to the murder of her son Jonathan in 2017, his attempted murder earlier that year, and attempted fraud.

items.title: Big Banana graffiti investigated as possible act of hate speech under new laws
items.summary: The graffiti, criticising Israel, was sprayed onto Coffs Harbour's famous Big Banana over the Easter long weekend.

items.title: Police allege P-plater was driving 54km over speed limit on beach over Easter
items.summary: Police crack down on beach driving these school holidays — alleging two minors were driving unlicensed and a P-plater was driving 54km/h over the speed limit in a 40km/h zone.

items.title: Australian War Memorial to review Ben Roberts-Smith display following war crimes charges
items.summary: The Australian War Memorial will review its display of Ben Roberts-Smith, who has been charged with committing war crimes.

items.title: Friends watch search for rock fisherman missing from NSW mid north coast
items.summary: Friends watched helplessly as a 32-year-old man was washed off Emerald Beach Headland, unable to swim to his rescue.

items.title: Police bodycam footage after nine people were arrested
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Townsville counting costs after crime spree sees nine arrested and police injured
items.summary: Daniel Toppi was just starting to get back on his feet before a group of teenagers allegedly stole his work vehicle. Police have arrested nine teenagers in relation to the incident that also included the alleged theft of a police vehicle.

items.title: Talia Gibson jumps at chance to spearhead Australia against Britain in Billie Jean King Cup
items.summary: Talia Gibson did not have to be asked twice to spearhead Australia's Billie Jean King Cup campaign against Great Britain, with the rising star happily putting the green and gold above her personal ambitions.

items.title: Brisbane Broncos name Reece Walsh replacement, Victor Radley returns from suspension
items.summary: Jesse Arthars has been named at fullback for the Brisbane Broncos ahead of Friday's blockbuster clash against the North Queensland Cowboys.

items.title: Aboriginal leaders Naomi Mayers and Ann Weldon remembered as trailblazers after dying days apart
items.summary: Naomi Mayers helped establish the first Aboriginal medical services in Australia, turning self-determination into a model of care across the country. Her death came days before Wiradjuri woman and "fierce trailblazer" Ann Weldon.

items.title: Canberra man jailed over 'substantially important' role in major illegal steroid and prescription drug syndicate
items.summary: A Canberra man is jailed for his part in an illegal drug ring which operated as an online pharmacy, distributing steroids and prescription drugs across the country.

items.title: Donald Trump's shifting rhetoric on Iran war works against his rapidly thinning credibility
items.summary: Donald Trump's increasingly erratic language means that a US president's word — which would have once been regarded with gravitas — holds perhaps only the same weight as the word of the "evil" regime he says he has obliterated several times.

items.title: Artemis II lunar fly-by breaks Apollo 13's distance record, reveals previously unseen far side of the Moon
items.summary: The astronauts have travelled further from Earth than any humans before, capturing never-before-seen views of the far side of the Moon.

items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Australia's EV sales surge in March amid growing fuel security fears, rising petrol prices
items.summary: EV sales have surged in March, a month into the war in Iran. But it is not the only signal indicating a move away from fuel cars.

items.title: Rapper Offset — Cardi B's ex-husband — in stable condition after being shot
items.summary: The Migos rapper is in a stable condition though his exact condition is unknown, according to his spokesperson.

items.title: NRL sends warning to Parramatta Eels captain Mitchell Moses for Easter Monday conduct
items.summary: The NRL has warned Parramatta captain Mitch Moses to tone down his aggression when speaking to referees following the fiery golden-point loss to Wests Tigers on Easter Monday.

items.title: 'Vegan leather' isn't as sustainable or eco‑friendly as brands might claim
items.summary: As fashion brands move away from animal leather, "vegan" has come to suggest something that is both kinder to animals and better for the planet. However, the reality is more complicated.

items.title: At-home stockpiling sparks safety warnings from fuel and fire experts
items.summary: Authorities are warning that improper or excessive storage of petrol or diesel in suburban homes carries a significant fire risk, as service station owners report excessive stockpiling.

items.title: Woman charged over alleged murder of Pheobe Bishop to face court again in May
items.summary: The case of a woman accused of murdering Queensland teenager Pheobe Bishop has been adjourned to May 15 — the first anniversary of Pheobe's disappearance.

items.title: Michael Voss blocking out Carlton criticism ahead of AFL Gather Round
items.summary: Carlton coach Michael Voss insists he is ignoring most of the intense scrutiny on his embattled AFL club, adamant key stakeholders remain on the same page.

items.title: Ben Roberts-Smith in custody at Silverwater jail after war crimes charges
items.summary: One of Australia's most decorated soldiers, Ben Roberts-Smith, has been charged with five counts of the war crime of murder.

items.title: Don't ask me about my love life. I'm busy writing a different story
items.summary: I might be on a different path, but I can still love and be loved in ways that don't require me to be a mother or a partner.

items.title: Inside Delhi's growing black market for cooking gas after Strait of Hormuz closure
items.summary: Across Delhi, people are waiting days and sometimes weeks for gas cylinders. Yet, the ABC has found a thriving black market, where the same gas can be bought quickly — for those who can afford it.

items.title: Police investigate 'suspicious' death on Barkly Highway after spate of Easter road crashes in NT
items.summary: NT Police are investigating the "suspicious death" of a woman on a remote stretch of highway in the early hours of Easter morning.

items.title: Australian Army reservist faces court after allegedly working for Ukraine military without authorisation
items.summary: An Australian Army reservist faces an Adelaide court after he allegedly worked for the Ukraine military as a drone operator without authorisation.

items.title: Fears for Ningaloo Reef after ex-cyclone decimates region amid coral spawning season
items.summary: As sharks swarm in the aftermath, there are fears ex-tropical Cyclone Narelle may have triggered a climate disaster for the world-famous Ningaloo Reef.

items.title: Queensland maritime authorities criticised for 'slow' wrecked boats salvage
items.summary: The vessel 7 Wonder has sat wrecked at Rose Bay in north Queensland for more than two months. Locals say they have been picking up rubbish from the boat daily, but maritime authorities say local salvage contractors are "stretched".

items.title: PVC prices soar amid petrol and gas supply issues as Iran war continues
items.summary: Conflict in the Middle East has disrupted petroleum and gas supply chains, causing PVC prices to skyrocket in Australia.

items.title: PM brings forward trip to Singapore to shore up fuel supply — as it happened
items.summary: Anthony Albanese confirms he will be heading to Singapore for a meeting with his counterpart Lawrence Wong later this week.

items.title: WA man considering legal action after being sent home from hospital when he needed surgery
items.summary: An Australian Navy candidate says he nearly lost his leg following delays in diagnosis and treatment of his emergency condition by two Perth hospitals.

items.title: Concern over 'permanent gridlock' as proposed Dreamworld expansion considered
items.summary: The state government will decide how to proceed with a proposed expansion of Dreamworld, but residents and experts want traffic gridlock to be considered.
